name: vaultwarden
description: Lightweight self-hosted password manager compatible with Bitwarden clients

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_PORT
fields.label: App Port
fields.help: Host port used to open Vaultwarden in your browser.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_DATA_LOCATION
fields.label: Vaultwarden Data Folder
fields.help: Stores your Vaultwarden database, attachments, sends, icons, and configuration data.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_DOMAIN
fields.label: Public Domain
fields.help:
Optional public URL including http:// or https://.
Example: https://vault.example.com.
Recommended when you access Vaultwarden through a reverse proxy or custom domain.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_SIGNUPS_ALLOWED
fields.label: Allow New Signups
fields.help: Controls whether new users can create accounts from the sign-up page. Most self-hosted Vaultwarden setups leave this disabled after the initial setup.

fields.name: RESTART_POLICY
fields.label: Restart Policy
fields.help: Controls whether the container restarts automatically.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_IMAGE
fields.label: Image
fields.help:
Docker image used for Vaultwarden.
Change this only if you want a different tag or image source.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_ADMIN_TOKEN
fields.label: Admin Token
fields.help:
Optional admin token for the Vaultwarden admin page.
Only set this if you intend to use the admin page, and use a long random secret.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_CONTAINER_NAME
fields.label: Container Name
fields.help: EasyDocker sets the container name from the project name.

fields.name: VAULTWARDEN_DATA_TARGET
fields.label: Data Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal path where Vaultwarden stores all persistent data.

ui.overview: Vaultwarden is a lightweight self-hosted password manager compatible with Bitwarden apps and browser extensions.
what_happens: A Vaultwarden container will be created
Your persistent data will be stored in the folder you choose
The web interface will be published on the port you choose
You can optionally allow signups, set a public domain, and enable the admin page token
ui.data_location: Relative paths are stored inside this app's project folder under the mounted EasyDocker base config path.
